meta.title: James Liu
meta.author: James Liu
meta.bio:
I'm a photographer based in San Francisco.

I shoot mostly on film, drawn to quiet moments and the way light falls differently in every city.

flip.markdown: Somewhere in Lisbon, 2022.

**Borough Market**

Early morning, before the crowds.
Barcelona from the roof of a stranger's apartment.
*He didn't move for twenty minutes.*

## Kowloon, 2019

The last trip before everything changed.
November in Cornwall. Not a soul around.

Bosphorus crossing, 6am.

I hadn't slept.
Paris does rain better than anywhere.

Somewhere between Chamonix and Geneva.

We were very lost.

**Chiang Mai**

The smell of lemongrass everywhere.

projects.title: Tokyo 2024
projects.story:
Three weeks in Japan in the dead of winter. I brought two rolls of Portra 400 thinking it would be enough.

It wasn't. I came back with fourteen.

Tokyo resists being photographed. Everything moves too fast, the light changes every few minutes, and the city has a way of making you feel like you're always in the wrong place. These are the moments I managed to hold onto.

flip.markdown:
## Shinjuku, 11pm

The rain had just stopped. Everyone moved like they had somewhere to be.

**Meiji Shrine**

Golden hour through cedar trees. I waited forty minutes for the crowd to thin.

A 12-seat counter in Shibuya. Opened in 1978.

- Tonkotsu broth
- Hand-cut noodles
- No English menu

## Yamanote Line

Every face a separate story, moving in parallel.

One of 5 million vending machines in Japan.

*Shot at ISO 3200, handheld.*

photos.alt: View from a rooftop in Nakameguro

flip.markdown: Nakameguro from above. Cherry blossoms two weeks out.

projects.title: SF Fog
projects.story:
San Francisco looks best when you can't quite see it.

The fog does something to the light here that I've never been able to fully explain — it flattens everything into a kind of soft grey that makes the color that *does* survive feel impossibly vivid.

flip.markdown:
## The Gate

You can hear it before you see it.
24th and Valencia. The mural changes every few years.

**Sutro Tower**

The city's antenna, always looming.

photos.alt: Dolores Park on a cloudy Sunday

flip.markdown: Dolores Park. Overcast, 58°F. Still packed.

Painted Ladies, without the tourists.

*Shot at 6am.*

## Glencoe

The clouds sat so low they ate the peaks.
Built in 1723. Still in daily use.
Loch Leven. The light lasted maybe four minutes.
*They stared back.*
